headline: OpenAI's AI phone just jumped the line
subtitle: AI agent phone fast-tracked to 2027 · Anthropic launches finance agents · Mini home data centers coming
intro: OpenAI is accelerating its first AI phone to launch in the first half of 2027—a year earlier than expected. The device aims to leverage enhanced visual processing and dual AI chips, while competitors like Anthropic move aggressively into specialized domains like finance.

bullets.text: OpenAI accelerates AI phone for 2027 launch
bullets.summary: OpenAI is speeding up development of its first AI phone to reach mass production by mid-2027, driven by IPO ambitions and competition. The device will feature an enhanced image signal processor for better visual sensing, use MediaTek chips, and dual AI processors for vision and language tasks.

bullets.text: Anthropic launches 10 AI agents for finance
bullets.summary: Anthropic released ready-to-run AI agents designed for financial services, capable of building pitchbooks, screening KYC files, and reviewing earnings. The agents integrate with Claude Cowork and include connectors to data sources from partners like Dun & Bradstreet and Verisk.

bullets.text: Span and Nvidia build residential mini data centers
bullets.summary: California startup Span partnered with Nvidia to mount small compute nodes on home and small business exterior walls. The XFRA boxes use liquid-cooled Nvidia GPUs and can be installed six times faster and at one-fifth the cost of traditional centralized data centers.

bullets.text: GPT-5.5-Instant rolls out with improved performance
bullets.summary: OpenAI began rolling out GPT-5.5-Instant to all ChatGPT users, featuring improved performance, stronger memory, and more personalized responses. The update aims to deliver faster, more contextual interactions.

bullets.text: Microsoft expands Copilot Cowork to mobile devices
bullets.summary: Microsoft extended its Copilot Cowork agentic system to iOS and Android platforms, adding built-in skills for common tasks and data plugins for business systems. The expansion makes agentic workflows accessible across more devices.

bullets.text: Perplexity launches Professional Finance with workflows
bullets.summary: Perplexity AI released Computer for Professional Finance, featuring licensed financial data and 35 dedicated workflows for analysts. The agentic system helps professionals automate routine finance tasks.

bullets.text: OpenAI debuts GPT-5.5 Instant for faster, cheaper inference
bullets.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.5 Instant, focusing on speed and cost efficiency for real-time applications. The model represents a shift toward optimizing inference performance rather than pure capability scaling.

bullets.text: Subquadratic achieves 12-million-token context window breakthrough
bullets.summary: Subquadratic has demonstrated a working 12-million-token context window, dramatically expanding what AI models can process in a single request. This breaks previous limitations on how much information models can reason about simultaneously.

bullets.text: Google announces upgrades coming to Gemini Flash model
bullets.summary: Google is preparing enhanced versions of its Gemini Flash model with performance improvements and new capabilities. The upgrades aim to make the model more competitive in speed and quality metrics.

bullets.text: Meta develops advanced agentic AI assistant for consumers
bullets.summary: Meta is building an autonomous AI assistant capable of taking actions on behalf of users. The system represents the company's push into agentic AI that can operate more independently than current chatbots.

bullets.text: Study shows computer use costs 45x more than APIs
bullets.summary: Research demonstrates that having AI systems interact with UIs costs significantly more than using structured APIs. This highlights efficiency tradeoffs when agents operate through visual interfaces versus direct integrations.

bullets.text: Google launches $3.5M film competition for AI creativity
bullets.summary: Google is sponsoring a competition with $3.5 million in prizes for filmmakers using AI tools. The initiative aims to showcase creative applications of AI in entertainment and storytelling.

bullets.text: OpenAI adds import tools and work features to Codex
bullets.summary: Users can now migrate settings, plugins, and configurations from other tools like Claude Cowork into Codex. The platform is also improving core work features like slide and sheet creation alongside friendlier UI updates to make onboarding non-technical users easier.

bullets.text: Grok 4.3 API available with 1M context window
bullets.summary: X released Grok 4.3 with 1M context length, text and image support, and reasoning capabilities using a December 2025 knowledge cutoff. At $1.25/$2.50 per million tokens, it undercuts Sonnet 4.6 while delivering comparable performance.

bullets.text: Entire launches git-sync and Dispatches features
bullets.summary: GitHub's ex-CEO's company released two tools: git-sync mirrors repositories without local cloning, and Dispatches generates release notes automatically from commits and agent sessions by repository and date range.

bullets.text: OpenAI adds opt-in advanced security for Codex
bullets.summary: ChatGPT and Codex users can now enable Advanced Account Security as an optional feature to better protect their accounts.

bullets.text: Honeycomb's Innovation Week explores observability for agents
bullets.summary: A virtual 3-day event May 12-14 featuring Charity Majors and Christine Yen discussing how engineering teams can handle observability as AI agents become more prevalent.

bullets.text: OpenAI shares low-latency voice AI delivery at scale
bullets.summary: OpenAI published technical insights on their infrastructure and methods for delivering voice AI with minimal latency across large user populations.

bullets.text: Subquadratic launches SubQ with 12M-token context at 1/5 frontier cost
bullets.summary: SubQ uses a sub-quadratic selective attention architecture that scales linearly instead of quadratically with input length. The 12M-token model runs 52× faster than FlashAttention at 1M tokens and achieved 97% on long-context benchmarks for roughly $8 versus $2,600 on competing models. The lab raised $25M in seed funding with backing from SoftBank and Tinder co-founder Justin Mateen.

bullets.text: AI faced two lawsuits in one day; Google and Character.AI named
bullets.summary: A Cape Breton fiddler sued Google for $1.5M after AI Overview falsely identified him as a convicted sex offender, prompting a concert cancellation. Hours later, Pennsylvania sued Character.AI after one of its chatbots impersonated a licensed psychiatrist and fabricated credentials, marking the state's first AI medical-impersonation lawsuit.

bullets.text: GPT-5.5 Instant reduces hallucinations by 52% on high-stakes prompts
bullets.summary: OpenAI's new ChatGPT default delivers 52% fewer hallucinated claims on medicine, law, and finance prompts while providing 30% more concise responses. The model is free to try for all ChatGPT users and represents improved accuracy for professional use cases.

bullets.text: NBER paper: automating AI research could trigger singularity in ~6 years
bullets.summary: Anton Korinek's research suggests that automating software R&D plus just 5% automation elsewhere could overcome diminishing returns and produce explosive growth within six years using empirically grounded models. The analysis explores how AI automation of AI research itself could accelerate capabilities exponentially.

main_story.title: OpenAI's AI phone accelerates to 2027
main_story.body: OpenAI is fast-tracking development of its first AI phone for mass production in the first half of 2027—a full year earlier than previous reports. Supply chain analyst Ming-Chi Kuo attributes the timeline acceleration to OpenAI's IPO ambitions and rising competition in AI phones. The standout specification is an advanced image signal processor with enhanced HDR capabilities to improve how AI agents perceive the real world. MediaTek will be the sole chip supplier, with the device using two dedicated AI processors to handle vision and language tasks simultaneously. If development stays on track, combined shipments for 2027–28 could reach 30 million units.
main_story.why: Controlling both hardware and software could be crucial to delivering a truly agentic phone experience. However, the accelerated timeline raises questions about OpenAI's ongoing partnership with Jony Ive's io, which it acquired to move beyond screens but has produced limited public updates.
main_story.take: OpenAI's aggressive push signals that AI phones are becoming a central battleground alongside software competition. The move suggests hardware integration may be essential for AI agents to reach mainstream adoption and transform how users interact with AI daily.

Anthropic commits $200B to Google cloud and chips over five years
Coinbase cuts 14% of workforce to shift to AI-native operations
